response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of broad software engineering experience and genuine staff-level credibility, but the resume reveals critical gaps against the core requirements for this role. The position demands 8+ years of production AI solution delivery; his substantive AI exposure is limited to roughly six months at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling) with no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. Python—a primary listed technology—does not appear in his skills or employment history. His cloud experience is real but narrow (AWS CDK at Optilyz for a pricing feature). The current career gap and concentration of AI work in his most recent, shortest tenure make it difficult to distinguish depth from surface familiarity at the staff level this role requires.
strengths: Genuine staff-level engineering tenure across multiple companies with a track record of taking on broad technical scope
Hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) server work and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq directly aligns with the role's MCP and agentic pipeline responsibilities
Strong TypeScript/Node.js background and demonstrated ability to build and operate high-throughput distributed systems (Dunnhumby, Optilyz)
concerns: Python is absent from the resume entirely despite being a primary language requirement for the role
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or named multi-agent frameworks—all required or strongly preferred qualifications
AI/GenAI experience is limited to one six-month role followed by a career gap, falling far short of the 8-year production AI delivery requirement

key_factors.factor: MCP server and agentic workflow contribution
